3654	\begin{lemma}
3655	[Product of short exact sequences in \(\mathrm{Ab}\) (AB4*)]
3656	  \label{lem:short_exact_pi_map}
3657	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.shortExact_piMap, AlgebraicGeometry.pi_π_map_apply}
3658	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
3659	  \textit{Project AB4* lemma (Archon-original infrastructure).}
3660	  Let \((S_j)_{j \in J}\) be a family, indexed by an arbitrary type \(J\), of short exact sequences
3661	  of abelian groups, \(S_j : 0 \to A_j \xrightarrow{f_j} B_j \xrightarrow{g_j} C_j \to 0\). Then
3662	  the short complex assembled from the product maps,
3663	  \[
3664	    0 \to \prod_{j \in J} A_j \xrightarrow{\;\prod_j f_j\;} \prod_{j \in J} B_j
3665	      \xrightarrow{\;\prod_j g_j\;} \prod_{j \in J} C_j \to 0,
3666	  \]
3667	  is again short exact. This is the AB4* exactness-of-products property of \(\mathrm{Ab}\); it is
3668	  the degreewise engine of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}, since each {\v C}ech term is a product
3669	  of basic-open section groups (Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex}).
3670	\end{lemma}
3671	\begin{proof}
3672	  Monomorphy of the product map \(\prod_j f_j\) is automatic, as products preserve monomorphisms.
3673	  Exactness at the middle is checked componentwise: an element of \(\prod_j B_j\) annihilated by
3674	  \(\prod_j g_j\) is annihilated in each coordinate \(j\), hence lies in the image of \(f_j\) by
3675	  exactness of \(S_j\), and the resulting coordinatewise preimages assemble to a preimage in
3676	  \(\prod_j A_j\). The one nontrivial point is epimorphy of \(\prod_j g_j\), which is \emph{not} an
3677	  automatic instance in \(\mathrm{Ab}\): given a target tuple in \(\prod_j C_j\), surjectivity of
3678	  each \(g_j\) supplies a coordinatewise preimage, and these assemble — through the canonical
3679	  identification of an \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-valued product with the set-theoretic product of its
3680	  components — to a preimage of the whole tuple. Hence \(\prod_j g_j\) is an epimorphism and the
3681	  displayed sequence is short exact.
3682	\end{proof}
3683	
3684	\begin{lemma}
3685	
3686	\leanok
3687	[Short exact sequence of {\v C}ech complexes from a basis]
3688	  \label{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}
3689	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechComplex_shortExact_of_basis}
3690	  \uses{def:cech_complex, def:face_short_complex, def:section_cech_short_complex,
3691	    lem:ses_cech_h1, lem:short_exact_pi_map}
3692	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01EO, Lemma
3693	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof, term-wise-product step) (read from
3694	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1695--1776).
3695	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "By Lemma \ref{lemma-ses-cech-h1} and our assumption (2)
3696	  % this sequence gives rise to an exact sequence
3697	  % $$
3698	  % 0 \to \mathcal{F}(U) \to \mathcal{I}(U) \to \mathcal{Q}(U) \to 0.
3699	  % $$
3700	  % for every $U \in \mathcal{B}$. Hence for any $\mathcal{U} \in \text{Cov}$
3701	  % we get a short exact sequence of {\v C}ech complexes
3702	  % $$
3703	  % 0 \to
3704	  % \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \to
3705	  % \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) \to
3706	  % \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{Q}) \to 0
3707	  % $$
3708	  % since each term in the {\v C}ech complex is made up out of a product of
3709	  % values over elements of $\mathcal{B}$ by assumption (1)."
3710	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01EO,
3711	  \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof).}
3712	  Fix an index family \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) (giving the covering \(\mathcal{U}\)) and a
3713	  short complex
3714	  \[
3715	    P : \mathcal{P}_1 \to \mathcal{P}_2 \to \mathcal{P}_3
3716	  \]
3717	  of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Assume that for every degree \(p\) and every tuple
3718	  \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) the per-face short complex of
3719	  Definition~\ref{def:face_short_complex} — the section sequence
3720	  \[
3721	    \mathcal{P}_1(V_\sigma) \longrightarrow \mathcal{P}_2(V_\sigma) \longrightarrow
3722	      \mathcal{P}_3(V_\sigma), \qquad V_\sigma = \bigcap_k U(\sigma k)
3723	  \]
3724	  — is short exact. Then the induced short complex of section {\v C}ech complexes of
3725	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_short_complex},
3726	  \[
3727	    0 \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_1) \to
3728	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_2) \to
3729	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}_3) \to 0,
3730	  \]
3731	  is short exact (as a short complex of cochain complexes of abelian groups).
3732	
3733	  \emph{Remark.} In the cover-global \((\mathcal{B}, \mathrm{Cov})\) setting of
3734	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}, where \(P\) is the section short complex of a short
3735	  exact sequence \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) of sheaves, the per-face
3736	  hypothesis is discharged by the per-face short-exact-sequence derivation of
3737	  Lemma~\ref{lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses}: left-exactness of the section sequence over \(V_\sigma\) is
3738	  automatic, and surjectivity \(\mathcal{I}(V_\sigma) \to \mathcal{Q}(V_\sigma)\) is the output of
3739	  Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1} at the basic open \(V_\sigma\) (condition (1) placing \(V_\sigma \in
3740	  \mathcal{B}\)).
3741	\end{lemma}
3742	
3743	\begin{proof}
3744	  \uses{def:section_cech_short_complex, def:face_short_complex, lem:short_exact_pi_map}
3745	  \leanok
3746	  Short-exactness of a short complex of cochain complexes is checked degreewise: it suffices to
3747	  verify short-exactness in each cohomological degree \(p\). In degree \(p\) the term of the
3748	  section {\v C}ech complex is the product \(\prod_{\sigma}(-)(V_\sigma)\) over all tuples
3749	  \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) of section groups over the basic opens \(V_\sigma\), and
3750	  the two maps are the products of the corresponding per-face maps; thus the degree-\(p\) short
3751	  complex is the product over \(\sigma\) of the per-face short complexes
3752	  (Definition~\ref{def:face_short_complex}). Each of these is short exact by hypothesis, so their
3753	  product is short exact by the AB4* product-of-short-exact-sequences lemma
3754	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:short_exact_pi_map}). As this holds in every degree, the short complex of section
3755	  {\v C}ech complexes (Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_short_complex}) is short exact.
3756	\end{proof}

3758	\begin{lemma}
3759	
3760	\leanok
3761	[Per-face short exact sequence from a sheaf short exact sequence]
3762	  \label{lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses}
3763	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.faceShortComplex_shortExact_of_sheaf_ses,
3764	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor}
3765	  \uses{lem:ses_cech_h1, def:face_short_complex}
3766	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01EO, Lemma
3767	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof, section-exactness step) (read from
3768	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1729--1734).
3769	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "By Lemma \ref{lemma-ses-cech-h1} and our assumption (2)
3770	  % this sequence gives rise to an exact sequence
3771	  % $$
3772	  % 0 \to \mathcal{F}(U) \to \mathcal{I}(U) \to \mathcal{Q}(U) \to 0.
3773	  % $$
3774	  % for every $U \in \mathcal{B}$."
3775	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01EO,
3776	  \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof).}
3777	  Let \(S : 0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) be a short exact sequence of
3778	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, let \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) be an index family,
3779	  and suppose that on every face \(V_\sigma = \bigcap_k U(\sigma k)\) the section map
3780	  \(\mathcal{I}(V_\sigma) \to \mathcal{Q}(V_\sigma)\) is surjective — the basis-surjectivity datum
3781	  supplied by Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1}. Writing \(P\) for the section short complex of \(S\)
3782	  regarded as a short complex of presheaves of modules, the per-face short complex of
3783	  Definition~\ref{def:face_short_complex} is then short exact for every degree \(p\) and tuple
3784	  \(\sigma\). This lemma produces the per-face hypothesis consumed by
3785	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}, and is the bridge by which
3786	  Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing} instantiates the cover-local lemmas from a
3787	  genuine sheaf short exact sequence.
3788	\end{lemma}
3789	\begin{proof}
3790	  \uses{lem:ses_cech_h1, def:face_short_complex}
3791	  \leanok
3792	  Fix \(p\) and \(\sigma\), and put \(V = V_\sigma = \bigcap_k U(\sigma k)\). Monomorphy of
3793	  \(\mathcal{F}(V) \to \mathcal{I}(V)\) and exactness at the middle of \(\mathcal{F}(V) \to
3794	  \mathcal{I}(V) \to \mathcal{Q}(V)\) are the left-exactness of the sections functor applied to the
3795	  short exact sequence of sheaves \(S\): the sections functor \(\mathcal{G} \mapsto \mathcal{G}(V)\)
3796	  is the composite of the inclusion of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules into presheaves of
3797	  modules (a right adjoint, hence preserving finite limits) with evaluation at \(V\) (which
3798	  preserves limits), so it is left exact and carries the exactness of \(S\) to exactness of the
3799	  section sequence at \(\mathcal{I}(V)\). The remaining map \(\mathcal{I}(V) \to \mathcal{Q}(V)\) is
3800	  surjective by hypothesis, i.e. by the output of Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1} at the basic open
3801	  \(V\). Hence the per-face short complex is short exact.
3802	\end{proof}

3804	\begin{lemma}
3805	
3806	\leanok
3807	[Quotient preserves vanishing positive homology (homological core)]
3808	  \label{lem:cech_homology_quotient_vanishing}
3809	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechHomology_quotient_vanishing}
3810	  \uses{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}
3811	  \textit{Abstract homological core of Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech}.}
3812	  Let \(T : 0 \to T_1 \to T_2 \to T_3 \to 0\) be a short exact sequence of cochain complexes
3813	  (cohomological \(\mathbb{N}\)-grading) of abelian groups, and suppose the middle term \(T_2\) and
3814	  the left term \(T_1\) both have vanishing positive homology: \(H^p(T_2) = 0\) and \(H^p(T_1) = 0\)
3815	  for all \(p > 0\). Then the right term \(T_3\) also has vanishing positive homology, \(H^p(T_3) =
3816	  0\) for all \(p > 0\). This is the section-Čech-free homological core consumed by
3817	  Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech}.
3818	\end{lemma}
3819	\begin{proof}
3820	  \uses{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}
3821	  \leanok
3822	  Fix \(p > 0\). In the homology long exact sequence of \(T\)
3823	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}) the two homology groups of the middle term
3824	  adjacent to the connecting map at \(T_3\) in degree \(p\), namely \(H^p(T_2)\) and \(H^{p+1}(T_2)\),
3825	  both vanish (positive degrees, by the hypothesis on \(T_2\)); hence the connecting homomorphism is
3826	  an isomorphism \(H^p(T_3) \cong H^{p+1}(T_1)\). The right-hand side vanishes since \(p + 1 > 0\)
3827	  and \(T_1\) has vanishing positive homology. Therefore \(H^p(T_3) = 0\).
3828	\end{proof}

3831	
3832	\leanok
3833	[Quotient preserves vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology]

## Goal

Prove `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` (`lem:cech_computes_cohomology`),
the protected, frozen-signature target: for `f : X ⟶ S` separated and quasi-compact, `F`
quasi-coherent, `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X`, an isomorphism in the weak existence
form `Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F)` (under
`[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]`), with `higherDirectImage f i F =
((pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F`. End-state: zero inline `sorry` in the cone, zero
project axioms, kernel-only axioms.

## Routes

### Route A — acyclic-resolution comparison (CHOSEN)
The augmented Čech complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` on `X` (`Cᵖ = ∏ (j_s)_*(F|_{U_s})`) is
(i) a resolution of `F` and (ii) termwise `(pushforward f)`-acyclic. The P4 abstract lemma
gives `Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱf_* F` directly — ONE abstract lemma, NO spectral sequences. Acyclicity
input (ii) reduces to affine Serre vanishing `H^q(affine, qcoh)=0`, supplied by the P3b bridge.

### Route B — two spectral sequences (REJECTED, fallback only)
Both absent from Mathlib (multi-thousand-LOC), and Leray needs quasi-coherence of `R^q f_* F`.
Rests on the same `injective_cech_acyclic` brick as A — rejecting B does not escape it.

### The acyclicity bridge (torsor-free, load-bearing)
Route A's acyclicity reduces to affine Serre vanishing `H^q(Spec A, qcoh)=0` (02KG). The honest
non-circular route is the minimal Čech↔derived bridge: (1) `injective_cech_acyclic`, (2)
`ses_cech_h1`, (3) the dimension-shift `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (01EO) consuming the
standard-cover Čech vanishing of P3 as its condition (3). All three bricks of (1)+(2) are done;
01EO is the next target. This breaks the circular regress: P3 produces standard-cover Čech
vanishing; the bridge lifts it to affine sheaf vanishing without ever using affine vanishing.

### Absolute cohomology realization — Ext of the corepresenting object (Form B)
`H^p(U, F) := Ext^p_{X.Modules}(jShriekOU U, F)` where `jShriekOU U := sheafify(free(yoneda U))`
is the corepresenting object of `F ↦ Γ(U,F) = F(U)` (`= j_!O_U` up to iso), built from the
already-shipped `freeYonedaHomEquiv` + `sheafificationAdjunction` (~50–80 LOC). The three facts
01EO consumes are then off-the-shelf with the SES staying in `X.Modules`: injective vanishing
`Ext.eq_zero_of_injective` (injective `I` as the 2nd arg — no restriction taken), covariant LES
`Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃` at fixed 1st arg `jShriekOU U`, and `H⁰ ≅ Γ(U,F)` via the
corepresentability chain. The discarded alternatives: Form A `Ext(O_U, F|_U)` needs
restriction-preserves-injectives (≈ a 200–500 LOC `j_!` functor); `Functor.rightDerived` has
free injective vanishing but no packaged LES; `Sheaf.H` is absent. Reversal signal: Ext
universe/smallness pain over `SheafOfModules` → fall back to Route γ (Čech colimit), never `Sheaf.H`.

4274	\begin{proof}
4275	  Sheafification is exact: as the left adjoint of the inclusion of sheaves into
4276	  presheaves it preserves all colimits, and on a site admitting sheafification it
4277	  additionally preserves finite limits, so it preserves both the kernels and the
4278	  cokernels through which homology is computed. An exact additive functor commutes with
4279	  the homology functor of a cochain complex; chasing this through the counit complex
4280	  isomorphism \(\operatorname{sheafify}(\iota K) \cong K\) (the inclusion–sheafification
4281	  adjunction counit, an isomorphism on sheaves) gives
4282	  \(H^i(K) \cong H^i(\operatorname{sheafify} K) \cong
4283	  \operatorname{sheafify}(H^i(K))\). The supporting bricks are the
4284	  additivity of sheafification, the counit complex isomorphism, and the
4285	  complex-level transport of the short-complex map–homology isomorphism along an
4286	  additive homology-preserving functor.
4287	\end{proof}
